title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Anxiety
intro: Herbal teas for Anxiety are a type of herbal remedy that has been used to treat anxiety and promote relaxation. They can treat anxiety by interacting with the body's natural neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and GABA, to calm the mind and body. Examples of herbal teas that are commonly used to treat anxiety include chamomile, which has a soothing effect due to its high concentration of apigenin, a natural relaxant; lavender, which calms the nervous system and promotes relaxation; passionflower, which has a sedative effect; lemon balm, which has a calming effect on the nervous system; and valerian root, which is known for its ability to promote deep sleep and relaxation. Additionally, other herbal teas such as peppermint, ginger, and ashwagandha are also used to treat anxiety due to their ability to reduce stress and promote relaxation.

preparations.herb_desc: Valeriana officinalis teas, also known as valerian root tea, have been used for centuries to treat anxiety due to their sedative and calming properties. The valerenic acid and valeranone present in this herbal preparation help to reduce anxiety by acting on the GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. The bioactive constituents of valerian root tea, including valerenic acid, valeranone, and isovaleric acid, contribute to its anxiolytic effects by modulating the neurotransmitter activity and enhancing the GABAergic system. Regular consumption of valerian root tea has been shown to provide benefits in reducing anxiety symptoms, improving sleep quality, and promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Valeriana officinalis root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the dried root to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day, as needed, to help manage anxiety.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Valeriana officinalis teas for anxiety exhibit anxiolytic and sedative effects, representing one of the most thoroughly investigated medicinal plant materials with potential benefits in the treatment of fear and anxiety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Valeriana officinalis teas can be used to treat anxiety, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties. It is recommended to use Valeriana officinalis teas in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption, especially before driving or operating heavy machinery, and to be cautious when combining it with other sedatives or medications to treat anxiety.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Valeriana officinalis tea 1-2 times a day
Start with small dosages (250-500mg) and adjust as needed
Use Valeriana officinalis tea for anxiety for 2-4 weeks
Combine with a balanced lifestyle, exercise, and relaxation techniques
Consult a healthcare professional before long-term use

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas, also known as lemon balm tea, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their calming properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat anxiety by promoting relaxation, reducing stress levels, and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Melissa officinalis, such as rosmarinic acid and apigenin, contribute to its anxiolytic effects by interacting with GABA receptors and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters. Regular consumption of Melissa officinalis teas has been shown to provide benefits in reducing anxiety symptoms, improving mood, and enhancing overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Steep the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of ethnopharmacology", Melissa officinalis teas for anxiety have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic effects, which are attributed to mechanisms such as AChE inhibitory activity, stimulation of the acetylcholine and GABAA receptors, and inhibition of matrix metalloproteinase-2, as validated by modern pharmacological studies.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications. To use Melissa officinalis teas safely, take them in moderation, avoid driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious when combining them with other herbal supplements or medications that have sedative effects.
preparation_usage: Start by drinking 1 cup of Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times daily.
Choose a quiet space to drink tea and reduce external stress.
Take slow deep breaths while drinking tea to calm your mind.
Drink tea 30 minutes before bedtime to promote relaxation and sleep.
Drink tea consistently for 2-4 weeks to notice anxiety reduction.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to exhibit anxiolytic effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters like GABA and serotonin. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels, Lavandula angustifolia teas help to alleviate anxiety symptoms, leading to improved sleep quality, reduced muscle tension, and enhanced overall well-being. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been associated with several benefits, including reduced anxiety ratings, improved mood, and increased feelings of calmness and relaxation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 tablespoons of dried flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Remove the pot from heat and add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, and drink 1-2 cups a day to help with anxiety.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Lavandula angustifolia teas may help relieve symptoms of mental stress and anxiety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be an effective herbal remedy for anxiety relief, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to consume Lavandula angustifolia teas in moderation, avoiding excessive consumption, especially before driving or operating heavy machinery, and being cautious when combining with other sedatives or medications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried flowers per cup of boiling water
Reduce anxiety with 250-500 mg of Lavandula angustifolia extract daily
Use Lavandula angustifolia tea for 2-6 weeks for noticeable effects
Consult a healthcare professional for personalized dosage and guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Passiflora incarnata teas have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their calming and sedative properties, which help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, particularly passiflorine, which are responsible for its anxiolytic effects. By interacting with the brain's GABA receptors, these compounds help to regulate the body's response to stress, leading to a decrease in anxiety symptoms. The benefits of Passiflora incarnata teas in treating anxiety include reduced symptoms of anxiety, improved sleep quality, and enhanced mood stability, making it a popular natural remedy for individuals seeking alternative solutions to manage anxiety.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers, or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water in a saucepan until it boils.
Add the gathered Passiflora incarnata to the boiling water, then reduce heat to low.
Let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain it into a cup.
Drink the tea when it's still warm, or let it cool down to room temperature before consumption.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current pharmaceutical biotechnology", Passiflora incarnata teas for anxiety showed one of the most positive results in clinical studies.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Passiflora incarnata teas can be an effective herbal remedy for anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, dizziness, and stomach upset in some individuals. To safely use Passiflora incarnata teas, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it in the afternoon or evening to minimize potential disruptions to sleep patterns.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Passiflora incarnata tea in the morning.
Take 1-2 teaspoons (5-10 ml) of dried herb per cup.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes or as directed.
Drink 1-2 cups per day, or as needed for anxiety relief.
Consult a healthcare professional for optimal dosage and usage.

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties. The bioactive constituents of Avena sativa, including avenacosides, avenanthramides, and beta-glucans, help to modulate the body's stress response and promote relaxation, thereby reducing anxiety symptoms. These bioactive compounds interact with the body's GABA receptors, promoting a sense of calm and tranquility, and also exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The regular consumption of Avena sativa teas can lead to improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for anxiety relief.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa root and place it in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Avena sativa root in the infuser or strainer.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or strainer.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink immediately. You can add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be used to treat anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and changes in blood pressure. Precautions to take when using Avena sativa teas include using it in moderation, avoiding it before driving or operating heavy machinery, and being aware of its potential interactions with other medications, particularly those used to treat anxiety or sleep disorders.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Avena sativa tea for anxiety.
Take 1-2 teaspoons of dried Avena sativa in a cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard solids.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed.
Continue using Avena sativa tea for 2-4 weeks to see anxiety relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used as a natural remedy to help alleviate anxiety and promote a sense of calmness. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba teas possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to their anxiolytic effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and GABA. The bioactive constituents, including quercetin, kaempferol, and bilobalide, work synergistically to reduce anxiety levels and improve mood by enhancing the activity of the brain's neurotransmitters and promoting a balance in the body's stress response. The benefits of consuming Ginkgo biloba teas for anxiety relief include improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in the infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Drink the tea and enjoy, or let it cool before refrigerating for later use.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the current scientific evidence, Ginkgo biloba teas for anxiety have shown a positive effect in reducing anxiety under pathological conditions, although more advanced clinical research is needed to confirm their efficacy.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can help alleviate anxiety symptoms in some individuals, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and headaches due to its potential interactions with medications or exacerbation of pre-existing conditions. Precautions when using Ginkgo biloba teas include being cautious when driving or operating heavy machinery, as they may impair coordination and reaction time, and also avoiding taking them with sedatives or antidepressants to minimize the risk of adverse interactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup of tea.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons if needed.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 4-6 weeks to observe anxiety relief.
Combine Ginkgo biloba tea with a balanced lifestyle and relaxation techniques.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been widely used to treat anxiety due to their unique properties and bioactive constituents. The sedative and anti-inflammatory properties of these teas, particularly the presence of gingerols and shogaols, help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of anxiety and stress. The bioactive constituents, such as 6-gingerol, have been shown to have a positive effect on GABA receptors, thereby reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can experience the benefits of reduced anxiety, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder, and a cup.
Add 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder to the cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the powder.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it slowly.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Indian journal of experimental biology", Zingiber officinale teas, made from the extracts of ginger, have been shown to exhibit significant anxiolytic activity, meaning they can help reduce anxiety, in mice models, particularly at doses of 50 and 200 mg/kg.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat anxiety, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase as needed, and avoid consuming them on an empty stomach or before surgery, as they may exacerbate bleeding and interact with blood thinners.
preparation_usage: Start with 1/2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale in 1 cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day as needed
Begin with low dosages and adjust as needed for anxiety relief
Combine with a balanced diet and regular exercise for best results

preparations.herb_desc: Humulus lupulus teas, also known as hop teas, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their anxiolytic and sedative properties, which help to calm the mind and body. The bioactive constituents present in hop teas, such as alpha-acid and iso-alpha-acid, possess a sedative effect that helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. By reducing stress and anxiety, hop teas provide a sense of calm and well-being, making it an effective herbal remedy for individuals suffering from anxiety. The benefits of using hop teas to treat anxiety include reduced symptoms of anxiety and improved sleep quality, making it a natural and holistic approach to managing this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Humulus lupulus flowers or 2 teaspoons of dried ones.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed. Store the tea in a glass bottle.
Drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed to help with anxiety.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Canadian journal of physiology and pharmacology, Humulus lupulus teas for anxiety may not be effective in alleviating anxiety as the study found that the aqueous extract of Humulus lupulus showed significant inhibition of glutamic acid decarboxylase (GAD) activity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Humulus lupulus teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating anxiety, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, digestive issues, and interactions with certain medications. When using Humulus lupulus teas to treat anxiety, it is essential to be cautious and take precautions, such as starting with small doses and avoiding use with other sedatives or substances that can exacerbate drowsiness.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been used to treat anxiety due to their anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties. The saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in this herbal preparation help to alleviate anxiety by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and regulating the body's response to anxiety. The flavonoids and saponins, particularly silibinin and silidianin, are bioactive constituents that contribute to the anxiolytic effects of Silybum marianum teas by interacting with the GABA receptors and modulating the activity of neurotransmitters. Regular consumption of Silybum marianum teas has been found to provide benefits in treating anxiety by reducing symptoms of anxiety and depression, improving mood, and promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Silybum marianum flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 2 tablespoons of honey (optional) and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice (optional).
Steep the Silybum marianum flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the flowers.
Add the honey and lemon juice (if using) to the tea and stir well.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat anxiety, but its use may be associated with side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its bitter and hepatotoxic properties. Precautions should be taken to avoid consuming large quantities or using it for extended periods, as this may exacerbate liver problems or interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and cholesterol-lowering medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis teas, also known as Baikal skullcap, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety and its related symptoms due to their calming and sedative properties. The flavonoids, particularly baicalein and baicalin, present in these teas help to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters and reduce stress levels, thereby alleviating anxiety. These bioactive constituents also possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to their therapeutic effects in reducing anxiety-related symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, Scutellaria baicalensis teas offer a natural and safe alternative for managing anxiety, with benefits including improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 tablespoon of dried Scutellaria baicalensis root.
Measure 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) for sweetness.
Place the dried root in a tea infuser or directly in the cup.
Pour the boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and add honey (if using) before drinking.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Scutellaria baicalensis teas can be used to treat anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using these teas to treat anxiety, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming them before driving or operating heavy machinery, as they may exacerbate drowsiness and impair cognitive function.

preparations.herb_desc: Hypericum perforatum teas, also known as St. John's Wort, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their adaptogenic and anxiolytic properties. The bioactive constituents, such as hyperforin and hypericin, help to modulate the activity of neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, thereby reducing anxiety symptoms. By promoting relaxation and calming the nervous system, Hypericum perforatum teas can help alleviate feelings of worry, fear, and restlessness associated with anxiety. The benefits of using Hypericum perforatum teas to treat anxiety include reduced stress levels, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Hypericum perforatum flowers or 1 teaspoon of dried flowers.
Combine the flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and transfer the tea to a clean cup.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day as needed to help with anxiety.
preparations.preparation_study: According to BMJ (Clinical research ed.), Hypericum perforatum teas for anxiety may be beneficial as the study found a significant advantage for Hypericum perforatum relative to imipramine in reducing anxiety-somatisation symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hypericum perforatum teas can be effective in treating anxiety, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset due to its interaction with certain medications and its ability to exacerbate conditions such as depression and bipolar disorder. To minimize side effects, it is essential to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming it with other stimulants, and be cautious when combining it with other herbal preparations or medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their adaptogenic and calming properties. The presence of L-theanine, an amino acid, and other bioactive constituents such as flavonoids and polyphenols, contribute to its anxiolytic effects by promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. By modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and influencing the brain's default mode network, Camellia sinensis teas help to alleviate anxiety symptoms and improve overall mood. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been reported to offer benefits in reducing anxiety-related symptoms, promoting better sleep, and enhancing cognitive function.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or oolong).
Heat 1 cup of water to 200°F (93°C) in a kettle or pot.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of Camellia sinensis leaves to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the heated water over the tea leaves and let it steep for 2-5 minutes, depending on the desired strength.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Serve hot or cold, sweetened or unsweetened.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Current pharmaceutical biotechnology, Camellia sinensis teas for anxiety showed promising effects in animal models, but more studies are needed to confirm its efficacy in treating anxiety disorders.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Camellia sinensis teas can provide relief from anxiety due to their calming effects, but possible side effects may include dizziness, headaches, and stomach upset in some individuals, particularly when consumed in excess or combined with other medications.

To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small amounts, gradually increasing the dosage as needed, and to avoid consuming Camellia sinensis teas before or during activities that require clear thinking, such as driving or operating heavy machinery.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas, also known as German chamomile tea, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their calming and soothing properties. The apigenin, luteolin, and bisabolol present in this herbal preparation help to reduce anxiety by interacting with the GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and calming effects. As a result, Matricaria chamomilla teas help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, such as restlessness and insomnia, by promoting a sense of calmness and reducing stress levels. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat anxiety include improved sleep quality, reduced anxiety symptoms, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water and 1 teaspoon of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers into a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Matricaria chamomilla flowers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste if desired.
Drink the tea slowly and calmly to help reduce anxiety. Repeat as needed, but not more than 3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Recent patents on biotechnology, Matricaria chamomilla teas for anxiety have been considered one of the medicinal plants used in Iran for the cure of stress and anxiety.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, particularly those used to treat depression or sedatives. To use Matricaria chamomilla teas safely, take it in moderation and avoid consuming it before driving or operating heavy machinery, as it can cause drowsiness and impair coordination.

preparations.herb_desc: Vitex agnus-castus teas have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their anxiolytic and adaptogenic properties, which help to reduce stress and promote emotional balance. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and lignans, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and hormones involved in anxiety regulation. By promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels, Vitex agnus-castus teas help to alleviate anxiety symptoms, such as restlessness, irritability, and difficulty concentrating. Regular consumption of this herbal preparation has been reported to provide benefits, including reduced anxiety frequency, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Vitex agnus-castus berries.
Choose a cup of boiling water and pour it over the Vitex berries in a teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the Vitex berries from the liquid.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 1-2 times a day, ideally in the morning and before bed, to help manage anxiety.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of women's health & gender-based medicine, Vitex agnus-castus teas have been shown to be effective in reducing anxiety symptoms as part of a comprehensive treatment for premenstrual syndrome (PMS), with a significant decrease in anxiety symptoms reported by 93% of patients after a treatment period of three menstrual cycles.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vitex agnus-castus teas can be beneficial in treating anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as dizziness, headaches, and nausea in some individuals. To minimize potential issues, it's essential to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid using Vitex agnus-castus teas if you're pregnant or breastfeeding, as it may stimulate uterine contractions and affect milk production.

preparations.herb_desc: Corydalis yanhusuo teas have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their sedative and anxiolytic properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as tetrahydroberberine, berberine, and protopine, which have been shown to exhibit a calming effect on the nervous system, helping to alleviate anxiety symptoms. These bioactive compounds interact with the brain's neurotransmitters to regulate stress response and promote relaxation, ultimately contributing to a reduced state of anxiety. The benefits of Corydalis yanhusuo teas in treating anxiety include improved sleep quality, reduced stress levels, and a sense of calmness, making them a popular herbal remedy for anxiety relief.

Add 1 teaspoon of Corydalis yanhusuo root powder to the water.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed to help with anxiety.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Corydalis yanhusuo teas can be effective in treating anxiety, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its sedative properties. To use Corydalis yanhusuo teas safely, take them in moderation and avoid consuming it in large quantities or combining it with other sedatives or medications, as this can increase the risk of adverse reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Lemna minor teas have been used as a natural remedy to treat anxiety, and its effectiveness can be attributed to its calming and anti-anxiety properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat anxiety by promoting relaxation, reducing stress, and improving sleep quality, thereby alleviating symptoms of anxiety. The bioactive constituents of Lemna minor teas, including flavonoids and alkaloids, contribute to its anxiolytic effects by interacting with neurotransmitters and regulating the body's stress response. The benefits of using Lemna minor teas to treat anxiety include reduced anxiety symptoms, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for anxiety relief.

Steep 2 tablespoons of Lemna minor leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to sweeten it, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, ideally before bed or when feeling anxious.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lemna minor teas can be used to treat anxiety, but it may also cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset, especially when consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with a low dose and gradually increase as needed, and to be cautious when combining with other medications or substances that may interact with the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their properties of promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. This herbal preparation helps to treat anxiety by modulating the body's response to stress through its sedative and anti-anxiety effects. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis, including alkaloids such as quinine and quinidine, contribute to its therapeutic properties. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat anxiety include reduced anxiety symptoms, improved mood, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Cinchona officinalis bark, a tea infuser or strainer, and a cup.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark per 8 oz of water.
Heat 8 oz of water in a pot until it starts boiling, then reduce heat.
Steep the Cinchona officinalis bark in the hot water for 5-7 minutes, then strain.
Drink the tea when it's still warm, ideally 1-2 cups per day as needed.

To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, take them in moderation, avoid taking them with other medications or substances that may interact with quinine, and be cautious of allergic reactions or sensitivity to the herbal preparation.

preparations.herb_desc: Piper methysticum teas, also known as kava tea, have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their sedative properties, which help to calm the mind and body. The bioactive constituents of kava, including kavalactones and flavokavains, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they interact with the brain's neurotransmitters to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. By reducing stress hormones and promoting a sense of calm, kava tea can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, such as restlessness and irritability. The benefits of using kava tea to treat anxiety include a natural and non-addictive approach, making it a popular alternative to pharmaceutical treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Piper methysticum root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 teaspoon of honey (optional) and set aside.
Steep the Piper methysticum root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the root.
Add honey if desired and drink the tea once it has cooled to a comfortable temperature.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, Piper methysticum teas for anxiety have been associated with anxiolytic activity in humans.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Piper methysticum teas can be used to treat anxiety, but possible side effects may include drowsiness, dizziness, and headaches, as well as interactions with other medications such as sedatives and antidepressants. To use these teas safely, it is essential to start with a small dose and gradually increase it, avoid driving or operating heavy machinery, and be cautious when consuming other substances that may exacerbate anxiety.

preparations.herb_desc: Ziziphus jujuba teas have been traditionally used to treat anxiety due to their adaptogenic and anxiolytic properties, which help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Ziziphus jujuba, including flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, contribute to its therapeutic effects by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA and serotonin, thus alleviating anxiety symptoms. Regular consumption of Ziziphus jujuba teas has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and promoting a sense of well-being. The benefits of using Ziziphus jujuba teas to treat anxiety include reduced symptoms of anxiety and improved overall mental health, making it a promising herbal remedy for individuals seeking a natural solution to manage anxiety.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ziziphus jujuba fruits.
Add 1 tablespoon of dried Ziziphus jujuba fruits or 2 tablespoons of fresh Ziziphus jujuba fruits to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Harefuah, Ziziphus jujuba teas for anxiety are not specifically mentioned in the given study, however the study does discuss various herbal teas and their effects, but it does not provide information on Ziziphus jujuba teas.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ziziphus jujuba teas can be used to treat anxiety, but it may cause side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and changes in blood sugar levels in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid consuming the tea before driving or operating heavy machinery, and monitor blood sugar levels closely, especially for those with pre-existing conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Schisandra chinensis teas have been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to treat anxiety ailments, due to their adaptogenic and antioxidant properties. These teas help to reduce anxiety by promoting relaxation, improving mood, and enhancing cognitive function, ultimately leading to a sense of calm and well-being. The bioactive constituents of Schisandra chinensis, including lignans, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, are responsible for its therapeutic effects, as they interact with neurotransmitters and hormones to regulate stress responses and emotional balance. By incorporating Schisandra chinensis teas into their daily routine, individuals can experience numerous benefits, including reduced anxiety levels, improved sleep quality, and enhanced overall mental well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Schisandra chinensis berries and a cup of boiling water.
Place the berries in a tea infuser or a small strainer and put it in a cup.
Pour the boiling water over the berries and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the berries.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and drink immediately.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Nutrients, Schisandra chinensis teas for anxiety may offer mental health benefits, as studies suggest that the interaction of its constituents with the gut microbiota could mediate these benefits.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Schisandra chinensis teas can cause potential side effects such as drowsiness, stomach upset, and allergic reactions when used to treat anxiety, as the active compounds may interact with other medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions. To use Schisandra chinensis teas safely, take precautions such as starting with low doses, avoiding concurrent use with sedatives or antidepressants, and monitoring your body's response to the herbal preparation.
